fact: The Woodstock Music and Art Fair began, drawing over 400,000 people to a dairy farm in Bethel, New York
The Beatles made their historic first appearance on The Ed Sullivan Show, watched by 73 million Americans
Michael Jackson released 'Thriller', which became the best-selling album of all time
MTV launched at 12:01 AM with 'Video Killed the Radio Star' by The Buggles
Live Aid concert began at Wembley Stadium in London to raise money for Ethiopian famine relief
Elvis Presley made his first appearance on The Ed Sullivan Show, watched by over 60 million viewers
The first Grammy Awards ceremony was held simultaneously in Beverly Hills and New York City
The first Rock and Roll Hall of Fame induction ceremony was held at the Waldorf-Astoria in New York
Queen released 'Bohemian Rhapsody', which would become one of rock's most iconic songs
The first Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival was held in Indio, California
Beyoncé released the visual album 'Lemonade' on HBO
Taylor Swift's Eras Tour began in Glendale, Arizona, becoming the highest-grossing tour ever
Adele released '25', which broke first-week album sales records in multiple countries
Nirvana released 'Nevermind', which would define the grunge movement
Prince released 'Purple Rain', the soundtrack to his semi-autobiographical film
Prince performed his iconic Super Bowl XLI halftime show in the pouring rain
Beyoncé headlined the Super Bowl XLVII halftime show with a Destiny's Child reunion
Pink Floyd released 'The Dark Side of the Moon', which stayed on the Billboard 200 for 937 weeks
Drake released double album 'Scorpion', breaking numerous streaming records
The Beatles released 'Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band'
Bob Dylan went electric at the Newport Folk Festival, shocking folk purists
Madonna performed 'Like a Virgin' at the first MTV Video Music Awards
Whitney Houston performed 'The Star-Spangled Banner' at Super Bowl XXV
Queen performed their legendary Live Aid set at Wembley Stadium
Kanye West interrupted Taylor Swift's acceptance speech at the MTV VMAs
Aretha Franklin released 'Respect', which became an anthem of the civil rights movement
The Rolling Stones played their first concert at the Marquee Club in London
Led Zeppelin released 'Led Zeppelin IV' featuring 'Stairway to Heaven'
Fleetwood Mac released 'Rumours', one of the best-selling albums ever
AC/DC released 'Back in Black', which became the second best-selling album of all time
Guns N' Roses released debut album 'Appetite for Destruction'
David Bowie released 'The Rise and Fall of Ziggy Stardust and the Spiders from Mars'
Bruce Springsteen released 'Born in the U.S.A.'
The Notorious B.I.G. released 'Ready to Die'
Tupac Shakur released 'All Eyez on Me'
Kendrick Lamar released 'good kid, m.A.A.d city'
Kendrick Lamar released 'To Pimp a Butterfly'
Taylor Swift released '1989', marking her transition from country to pop
Beyoncé surprise-released her self-titled visual album with no prior announcement
Billie Eilish released debut album 'When We All Fall Asleep, Where Do We Go?'
Frank Ocean released 'Blonde' exclusively on Apple Music
Rihanna released 'Anti' after years of delays
Bad Bunny released 'Un Verano Sin Ti'
Bruno Mars and Anderson .Paak released Silk Sonic's 'An Evening with Silk Sonic'

Arctic Monkeys released debut album 'Whatever People Say I Am, That's What I'm Not'
Vampire Weekend released self-titled debut album

Fleet Foxes released self-titled debut album
Lana Del Rey released 'Born to Die'
Charli XCX released 'how i'm feeling now' during lockdown
Jack Harlow released 'Come Home the Kids Miss You'
Lil Nas X's 'Old Town Road' began its record 19-week run at No. 1 on Billboard Hot 100
Michael Jackson debuted the moonwalk during 'Billie Jean' on Motown 25
Shakira and Jennifer Lopez headlined the Super Bowl LIV halftime show
The Weeknd headlined the Super Bowl LV halftime show
Rihanna headlined the Super Bowl LVII halftime show, revealing her pregnancy
Usher headlined the Super Bowl LVIII halftime show in Las Vegas
Britney Spears released '...Baby One More Time'
